title: chaenopleura quadrangularis
intro: Chaenopleura quadrangularis, also known as the West Indian cherry, is a tropical plant that belongs to the Rubiaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating gastrointestinal issues and fever, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Chaenopleura quadrangularis include reducing inflammation, relieving digestive problems, and acting as an antioxidant. Chaenopleura quadrangularis can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
